PROCEDURE NOTES — GASTROENTEROLOGY
Patient: Harris, Dorothy | DOB: 08/11/1952 | MRN: MRN89012765
Date of Procedure: 10/22/2025
Physician: Dr. George Martin, MD — Gastroenterology Specialists

PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with biopsy

INDICATIONS:
1. K21.0 — Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with esophagitis
2. K44.9 — Diaphragmatic hernia without obstruction or gangrene
3. K57.30 — Diverticulosis of large intestine without perforation or abscess

HISTORY:
Ms. Harris is a 72-year-old female with a long history of GERD refractory to maximum-dose PPI therapy (omeprazole 40mg BID x 12 weeks). She reports persistent heartburn, regurgitation, dysphagia to solids, and unintentional weight loss of 8 lbs over 3 months. She also has a known diaphragmatic hernia. Prior barium swallow (09/2025) showed a 4cm sliding hiatal hernia with reflux. Given her age, alarm symptoms (dysphagia, weight loss), and refractory GERD, diagnostic EGD was indicated per ACG guidelines.

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
1. CPT 43235 — EGD, diagnostic, upper GI endoscopy
2. CPT 43239 — EGD with biopsy, single or multiple
3. J1100 — Injection, dexamethasone sodium phosphate, 1 mg (administered for laryngeal edema prophylaxis)

FINDINGS:
- Esophagus: Grade C esophagitis (Los Angeles classification) wi

args.text: Triage disposition: Route to Clinical Appeals. Anthem Blue Cross Medicare Advantage denied CLM-2025-00016/DEN-016 as CO-50 medical necessity (remarks N657/N386) for DOS 10/22/2025; billed $10,937.51, paid $0, remaining $8,750; appeal deadline 03/12/2026. Lines denied include CPT 43235 diagnostic EGD, CPT 43239 EGD with biopsy, and J1100. Diagnosis support on claim includes K21.0 GERD with esophagitis (primary) and K44.9 diaphragmatic hernia. Procedure note reviewed: 72-year-old with long-standing GERD refractory to maximum-dose PPI therapy (omeprazole 40 mg BID x12 weeks), persistent heartburn/regurgitation, dysphagia to solids, unintentional 8-lb weight loss over 3 months, known hiatal/diaphragmatic hernia, and prior barium swallow showing 4 cm sliding hiatal hernia with reflux. Note states diagnostic EGD was indicated per ACG guidelines due to age, alarm symptoms, and refractory GERD; visible findings include Grade C esophagitis. This appears clinically supportable rather than a coding/entity/patient-balance issue, so route for clinical appeal with procedure note and supporting guideline/medical necessity argument.

args.text: Route to Clinical Appeals. CO-50 medical necessity denial for Anthem Medicare Advantage CLM-2025-00016, DOS 10/22/2025, remaining $8,750, deadline 03/12/2026. Denied lines: 43235 diagnostic EGD, 43239 biopsy, J1100. Clinical note supports medical necessity: age 72 with refractory GERD despite omeprazole 40 mg BID x12 weeks, dysphagia, 8-lb unintentional weight loss, known 4 cm sliding hiatal/diaphragmatic hernia with reflux, and Grade C esophagitis; note states EGD indicated per ACG guidelines. Not a coding/entity/patient-balance issue; appeal should include procedure note and guideline-based medical necessity support.
